toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with blame-crash for crash dump collection

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run dotnet test with blame-crash for crash dump collection

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to run tests with crash dump collection. In .NET, crash dumps can be collected using environment variables for the crash dump collection. The standard way to do this is:

1. Set `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1` to enable mini dumps on crash
2. Set `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4` for a full dump (or other values for different dump types)
3. Set `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName` to control the dump file path/name

Alternatively, you can use `dotnet test` with the `--blame-crash` option which uses the blame data collector to collect a crash dump when the test host crashes.


Since this is an MSTest project with `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`, the `--blame-crash` flag is the most straightforward approach as it's a built-in feature of `dotnet test`. I'll run the tests with that option enabled.

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with blame-crash for crash dump collection

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-21d41947ea2646a888a8c713de082494 && dotnet test --blame-crash 2>&1", "description": "Run dotnet test with blame-crash for crash dump collection", "initial_wait": 60}

data.content:
All 3 tests passed. The command to run tests with crash dump collection is:

```
dotnet test --blame-crash
```

The `--blame-crash` flag enables the blame data collector, which will automatically capture a full crash dump if the test host process crashes. Dumps are saved under `TestResults/` in the project directory. No crashes occurred in this run, so no dumps were generated.
